title: Dockerfile and Compose Validation, Image Tests, Fault Injection, and Quality Gates · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Clean Host Transfer
summary: A release builds successfully but fails at runtime on a clean host, ships a root default, and loses data when its dependency stalls. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker clean host transfer increment in container release test and fault matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify pull-by-digest, clean-cache build, clean-host startup, state recovery, architecture, shutdown, security, and rollback outside the developer machine.
Inject slow start, crash, dependency refusal, DNS failure, read-only storage, full disk, termination, and resource limits into an isolated Compose project.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Clean Host Transfer · Theory Docker Clean Host Transfer
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Clean Host Transfer contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit. Apply that move specifically to Docker Clean Host Transfer while producing container release test and fault matrix.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown. Competency probe: Verify pull-by-digest, clean-cache build, clean-host startup, state recovery, architecture, shutdown, security, and rollback outside the developer machine. A defensible solution applies Docker Clean Host Transfer, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to container release test and fault matrix.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-clean-host-transfer — Verify pull-by-digest, clean-cache build, clean-host startup, state recovery, architecture, shutdown, security, and rollback outside the developer machine.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-testing-validation-quality-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-testing-validation-quality-3
    artifact: container-test-plan.yaml
    decision: worked docker testing validation quality 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      static: dockerfile-and-compose-validation
      image-contract: user-files-entrypoint-health
      integration: dependency-and-fault-injection
      transfer: clean-host-pull-and-run
      negative: readonly-slow-stop-missing-secret
    verify: docker buildx build --check . && docker compose config --quiet
    failure: Reject a successful build as runtime proof, a single happy-path curl, mutable local cache, or tests that depend on undeclared host state.
    repair: Layer structural, image, runtime, integration, fault, clean-host, and supply-chain checks with reproducible fixtures and teardown.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A local cached success proves another host can pull and reproduce the release. This fails the Docker Clean Host Transfer contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

# Evidence: docker-clean-host-transfer-1soidh
  - competency: docker-clean-host-transfer
    artifact: container-test-plan.yaml
    decision: clean host transfer
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: native amd64 engine with a cold content store
    fault-injection: move a tag while the immutable digest remains available
    retained-proof: restored checksum and application-level consistency query
    case-key: 1soidh
    change: |
      static: dockerfile-and-compose-validation
      image-contract: user-files-entrypoint-health
      integration: dependency-and-fault-injection
      transfer: clean-host-pull-and-run

    failure: Reject a successful build as runtime proof, a single happy-path curl, mutable local cache, or tests that depend on undeclared host state.
